children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Director may fix the number of, and appoint and direct, all employees of the Bureau, in accordance with the applicable provisions of title 5.

children.heading: Employees of the Bureau
children.label: B Employees of the Bureau
children.text: The Director is authorized to employ attorneys, compliance examiners, compliance supervision analysts, economists, statisticians, and other employees as may be deemed necessary to conduct the business of the Bureau. Unless otherwise provided expressly by law, any individual appointed under this section shall be an employee as defined in and subject to the provisions of such title and other laws generally applicable to the employees of an Executive agency.section 2105 of title 5

children.label: I
children.text: fair, credible, and transparent methods of establishing qualification requirements for, recruitment for, and appointments to positions;

children.label: II
children.text: fair and open competition and equitable treatment in the consideration and selection of individuals to positions;

children.label: III
children.text: fair, credible, and transparent methods of assigning, reassigning, detailing, transferring, and promoting employees.

children.heading: Veterans preferences
children.label: ii Veterans preferences
children.text: In implementing this subparagraph, the Director shall comply with the provisions of section 2302(b)(11), regarding veterans’ preference requirements, in a manner consistent with that in which such provisions are applied under chapter 33 of title 5. The authority under this subparagraph to waive the requirements of that chapter 33 shall expire 5 years after .1July 21, 2010

children.label: A
children.text: The rates of basic pay for all employees of the Bureau may be set and adjusted by the Director.

children.label: B
children.text: The Director shall at all times provide compensation (including benefits) to each class of employees that, at a minimum, are comparable to the compensation and benefits then being provided by the Board of Governors for the corresponding class of employees.

children.label: C
children.text: All such employees shall be compensated (including benefits) on terms and conditions that are consistent with the terms and conditions set forth in section 248() of this title.l

children.heading: Bureau participation in Federal Reserve System Retirement Plan and Federal Reserve System Thrift Plan
children.label: 3 Bureau participation in Federal Reserve System Retirement Plan and Federal Reserve System Thrift Plan
i
children.text: both the Federal Reserve System Retirement Plan and the Federal Reserve System Thrift Plan, under the same terms on which such participation is offered to employees of the Board of Governors who participate in such plans and under the terms and conditions specified under ; orsection 5584(i)(1)(C) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the Civil Service Retirement System under chapter 83 of title 5 or the Federal Employees Retirement System under chapter 84 of title 5, if previously covered under one of those Federal employee retirement systems.

children.heading: Election period
children.label: B Election period
children.text: Bureau employees shall make an election under this paragraph not later than 1 year after the date of appointment by, or transfer under part F to, the Bureau. Participation in, and benefit accruals under, any other retirement plan established or maintained by the Federal Government shall end not later than the date on which participation in, and benefit accruals under, the Federal Reserve System Retirement Plan and Federal Reserve System Thrift Plan begin.

children.heading: Employer contribution
children.label: C Employer contribution
children.text: The Bureau shall pay an employer contribution to the Federal Reserve System Retirement Plan, in the amount established as an employer contribution under the Federal Employees Retirement System, as established under chapter 84 of title 5 for each Bureau employee who elects to participate in the Federal Reserve System Retirement Plan. The Bureau shall pay an employer contribution to the Federal Reserve System Thrift Plan for each Bureau employee who elects to participate in such plan, as required under the terms of such plan.

children.heading: Controlled group status
children.label: D Controlled group status
children.text: The Bureau is the same employer as the Federal Reserve System (as comprised of the Board of Governors and each of the 12 Federal reserve banks prior to ) for purposes of subsections (b), (c), (m), and () of .July 21, 2010osection 414 of title 26

children.heading: Labor-management relations
children.label: 4 Labor-management relations
children.text: Chapter 71 of title 5 shall apply to the Bureau and the employees of the Bureau.

children.heading: Establishment required
children.label: A Establishment required
children.text: Not later than 180 days after the designated transfer date, the Bureau shall appoint an ombudsman.

children.label: i
children.text: act as a liaison between the Bureau and any affected person with respect to any problem that such party may have in dealing with the Bureau, resulting from the regulatory activities of the Bureau; and

children.label: ii
children.text: assure that safeguards exist to encourage complainants to come forward and preserve confidentiality.

children.label: A
children.text: developments in markets for consumer financial products or services, including market areas of alternative consumer financial products or services with high growth rates and areas of risk to consumers;

children.label: B
children.text: access to fair and affordable credit for traditionally underserved communities;

children.label: C
children.text: consumer awareness, understanding, and use of disclosures and communications regarding consumer financial products or services;

children.label: D
children.text: consumer awareness and understanding of costs, risks, and benefits of consumer financial products or services;

children.label: E
children.text: consumer behavior with respect to consumer financial products or services, including performance on mortgage loans; and

children.label: F
children.text: experiences of traditionally underserved consumers, including un-banked and under-banked consumers.

children.heading: Community affairs
children.label: 2 Community affairs
children.text: The Director shall establish a unit whose functions shall include providing information, guidance, and technical assistance regarding the offering and provision of consumer financial products or services to traditionally underserved consumers and communities.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Director shall establish a unit whose functions shall include establishing a single, toll-free telephone number, a website, and a database or utilizing an existing database to facilitate the centralized collection of, monitoring of, and response to consumer complaints regarding consumer financial products or services. The Director shall coordinate with the Federal Trade Commission or other Federal agencies to route complaints to such agencies, where appropriate.

children.label: i
children.text: the State agency system has the functional capacity to receive calls or electronic reports routed by the Bureau systems;

children.label: ii
children.text: the State agency has satisfied any conditions of participation in the system that the Bureau may establish, including treatment of personally identifiable information and sharing of information on complaint resolution or related compliance procedures and resources; and

children.label: iii
children.text: participation by the State agency includes measures necessary to provide for protection of personally identifiable information that conform to the standards for protection of the confidentiality of personally identifiable information and for data integrity and security that apply to the Federal agencies described in subparagraph (D).

children.heading: Reports to the Congress
children.label: C Reports to the Congress
children.text: The Director shall present an annual report to Congress not later than March 31 of each year on the complaints received by the Bureau in the prior year regarding consumer financial products and services. Such report shall include information and analysis about complaint numbers, complaint types, and, where applicable, information about resolution of complaints.

children.heading: Data sharing required
children.label: D Data sharing required
children.text: To facilitate preparation of the reports required under subparagraph (C), supervision and enforcement activities, and monitoring of the market for consumer financial products and services, the Bureau shall share consumer complaint information with prudential regulators, the Federal Trade Commission, other Federal agencies, and State agencies, subject to the standards applicable to Federal agencies for protection of the confidentiality of personally identifiable information and for data security and integrity. The prudential regulators, the Federal Trade Commission, and other Federal agencies shall share data relating to consumer complaints regarding consumer financial products and services with the Bureau, subject to the standards applicable to Federal agencies for protection of confidentiality of personally identifiable information and for data security and integrity.

children.heading: Office of Fair Lending and Equal Opportunity
children.label: c Office of Fair Lending and Equal Opportunity

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Director shall establish within the Bureau the Office of Fair Lending and Equal Opportunity.

children.label: A
children.text: providing oversight and enforcement of Federal laws intended to ensure the fair, equitable, and nondiscriminatory access to credit for both individuals and communities that are enforced by the Bureau, including the Equal Credit Opportunity Act [ et seq.] and the Home Mortgage Disclosure Act [ et seq.];15 U.S.C. 169112 U.S.C. 2801

children.label: B
children.text: coordinating fair lending efforts of the Bureau with other Federal agencies and State regulators, as appropriate, to promote consistent, efficient, and effective enforcement of Federal fair lending laws;

children.label: C
children.text: working with private industry, fair lending, civil rights, consumer and community advocates on the promotion of fair lending compliance and education; and

children.label: D
children.text: providing annual reports to Congress on the efforts of the Bureau to fulfill its fair lending mandate.

children.label: A
children.text: shall be appointed by the Director; and

children.label: B
children.text: shall carry out such duties as the Director may delegate to such Assistant Director.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Director shall establish an Office of Financial Education, which shall be responsible for developing and implementing initiatives intended to educate and empower consumers to make better informed financial decisions.

children.label: A
children.text: financial counseling, including community-based financial counseling, where practicable;

children.label: B
children.text: information to assist with the evaluation of credit products and the understanding of credit histories and scores;

children.label: C
children.text: savings, borrowing, and other services found at mainstream financial institutions;

children.label: i
children.text: prepare the consumer for educational expenses and the submission of financial aid applications, and other major purchases;

children.label: ii
children.text: reduce debt; and

children.label: iii
children.text: improve the financial situation of the consumer;

children.label: E
children.text: assistance in developing long-term savings strategies; and

children.label: F
children.text: wealth building and financial services during the preparation process to claim earned income tax credits and Federal benefits.

children.label: A
children.text: working with the Community Affairs Office to implement the strategy to improve financial literacy of consumers; and

children.label: B
children.text: working with the research unit established by the Director to conduct research related to consumer financial education and counseling.

children.label: A
children.text: the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs of the Senate; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Committee on Financial Services of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Study and report on financial literacy program
children.label: 7 Study and report on financial literacy program
I
children.text: a potential certification process and standards for certification;

children.label: II
children.text: appropriate certifying entities;

children.label: III
children.text: resources required for funding such a process; and

children.label: IV
children.text: a cost-benefit analysis of such certification;

children.label: ii
children.text: technological resources intended to collect, analyze, evaluate, or promote financial literacy and counseling programs;

children.label: iii
children.text: effective methods, tools, and strategies intended to educate and empower consumers about personal finance management; and

children.label: iv
children.text: recommendations intended to encourage the development of programs that effectively improve financial education outcomes and empower consumers to make better informed financial decisions based on findings.

children.heading: Report
children.label: B Report
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Comptroller General of the United States shall submit a report on the results of the study conducted under this paragraph to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs of the Senate and the Committee on Financial Services of the House of Representatives.July 21, 2010

children.heading: Office of Service Member Affairs
children.label: e Office of Service Member Affairs
A
children.text: educate and empower service members and their families to make better informed decisions regarding consumer financial products and services;

children.label: B
children.text: coordinate with the unit of the Bureau established under subsection (b)(3), in order to monitor complaints by service members and their families and responses to those complaints by the Bureau or other appropriate Federal or State agency; and

children.label: C
children.text: coordinate efforts among Federal and State agencies, as appropriate, regarding consumer protection measures relating to consumer financial products and services offered to, or used by, service members and their families.

children.heading: Regional services
children.label: A Regional services
children.text: The Director is authorized to assign employees of the Bureau as may be deemed necessary to conduct the business of the Office of Service Member Affairs, including by establishing and maintaining the functions of the Office in regional offices of the Bureau located near military bases, military treatment facilities, or other similar military facilities.

children.heading: Agreements
children.label: B Agreements
children.text: The Director is authorized to enter into memoranda of understanding and similar agreements with the Department of Defense, including any branch or agency as authorized by the department, in order to carry out the business of the Office of Service Member Affairs.

children.heading: Definition
children.label: 3 Definition
children.text: As used in this subsection, the term “service member” means any member of the United States Armed Forces and any member of the National Guard or Reserves.

children.heading: Timing
children.label: f Timing
children.text: The Office of Fair Lending and Equal Opportunity, the Office of Financial Education, and the Office of Service Member Affairs shall each be established not later than 1 year after the designated transfer date.

children.heading: Office of Financial Protection for Older Americans
children.label: g Office of Financial Protection for Older Americans

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: Before the end of the 180-day period beginning on the designated transfer date, the Director shall establish the Office of Financial Protection for Older Americans, the functions of which shall include activities designed to facilitate the financial literacy of individuals who have attained the age of 62 years or more (in this subsection, referred to as “seniors”) on protection from unfair, deceptive, and abusive practices and on current and future financial choices, including through the dissemination of materials to seniors on such topics.

children.heading: Assistant director
children.label: 2 Assistant director
children.text: The Office of Financial Protection for Older Americans (in this subsection referred to as the “Office”) shall be headed by an assistant director.

children.label: i
children.text: help seniors recognize warning signs of unfair, deceptive, or abusive practices, protect themselves from such practices;

children.label: ii
children.text: provide one-on-one financial counseling on issues including long-term savings and later-life economic security; and

children.label: iii
children.text: provide personal consumer credit advocacy to respond to consumer problems caused by unfair, deceptive, or abusive practices;

children.label: B
children.text: monitor certifications or designations of financial advisors who advise seniors and alert the Commission and State regulators of certifications or designations that are identified as unfair, deceptive, or abusive;

children.label: i
children.text: disseminating information regarding the legitimacy of certifications of financial advisers who advise seniors;

children.label: ii
children.text: methods in which a senior can identify the financial advisor most appropriate for the senior’s needs; and

children.label: iii
children.text: methods in which a senior can verify a financial advisor’s credentials;

children.label: i
children.text: protecting themselves from unfair, deceptive, and abusive practices;

children.label: ii
children.text: long-term savings; and

children.label: iii
children.text: planning for retirement and long-term care;

children.label: E
children.text: coordinate consumer protection efforts of seniors with other Federal agencies and State regulators, as appropriate, to promote consistent, effective, and efficient enforcement; and

children.label: F
children.text: work with community organizations, non-profit organizations, and other entities that are involved with educating or assisting seniors (including the National Education and Resource Center on Women and Retirement Planning).

children.heading: Application of chapter 10 of title 5
children.label: h Application of chapter 10 of title 5
children.text: Notwithstanding any provision of chapter 10 of title 5, such chapter shall apply to each advisory committee of the Bureau and each subcommittee of such an advisory committee.
